site.title: My Father,
site.name: James A. Schroer
site.intro: Fragments from home movies, photographs, and the things I remember him saying.
site.footer: For the family · Made with love

blocks.quote: Trying to handle the truth of loss is human and infinitesimally specific to tiime and said humans involved. This media archive has helped me tremendously, it's also deepened my grattitude for it's existence in 2026. So many thing had to alighn for me to have this catharsis via home movies, over so many decades and yet here it is. And I'm so thankful to my grandfathers for setting everything in motion when only young men, after the secoond World War both picked up photography as a hobby and out of technical curiosity. Apparently it's in the Schroer DNA and the Hilton mix only gave us super powers.

blocks.caption: the tail end of the Super 8 era, but i'm so grateful my dad turned the cam on Grandpa Lee in that specific pose. The conenentration. The gravitas of any moment was elevlatee when he cra nked that camera. I can still stmell the leather light-metter pouch too.
